danger_signals.visual_form: Animal sprites (birds, unused enemies in moon levels)
danger_signals.meaning: Obstacles to navigate; targets for block-throwing mechanics

danger_signals.visual_form: Character sprite of the thief antagonist
danger_signals.meaning: Primary narrative threat; stealer of Ronald's Magic Bag

danger_signals.visual_form: Volcanic level design and color palette
danger_signals.meaning: Dangerous location tier; final confrontation zone with Hamburglar

danger_signals.visual_form: Projectile animations (tied flag, magic wand, rabbit in hat)

safety_signals.visual_form: Ronald McDonald, Birdie, Grimace, Professor, CosMc sprites
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones; quest givers; progression markers; allies in narrative

safety_signals.visual_form: Golden Arches and McDonald's franchise imagery icons
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward goal; unlocks path advancement; story progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Block items and collectible objects scattered in levels
safety_signals.meaning: Resource accumulation for player empowerment and survival
Home base; quest origin point; narrative anchor

faction_indicators.faction: McDonaldland allies (Ronald, Birdie, Grimace, Professor, CosMc)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Clubhouse, treehouse, loft, workshop, moon location, volcano approach

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Thief character with striped visual identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cliffs, Highlands near Grimace's loft, volcano final location
Distributed across levels; more prevalent in moon and volcano stages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Storybook opening cutscene; Ronald's clubhouse as starting environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players begin searching for puzzle cards; NPCs provide directional guidance

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early quest progression (four puzzle cards collected)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ronald's clubhouse environment; access to Birdie's treehouse location
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ronald directs players toward Birdie; next stage unlocked

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mid-quest progression (nine puzzle cards collected across stages)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition from natural settings (treehouse, loft) to technological (workshop, moon)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Professor introduces rocket; escalation of challenge and environmental variety

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Moon environment with CosMc; distinct visual style from earlier stages
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: CosMc directs toward volcano; hint of final confrontation imminent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Volcanic terrain; Hamburglar presence; Magic Bag as boss entity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hamburglar reveals bag escaped; Magic Bag launches attack projectiles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Return to Ronald; narrative closure cutscene
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Magic Bag defeated; item returned; game ends

environmental_storytelling.example: Ronald's clubhouse (starting point), Birdie's treehouse, Grimace's loft, Professor's workshop each serve as narrative waypoints and visual indicators of story progression
Meadow → treehouse → highlands → workshop → moon → volcano creates visual escalation of stakes and danger
Different characters hold varying numbers of cards (Ronald 4, Birdie 5, Grimace 3, Professor 5, CosMc 5, Hamburglar 6) indicating their narrative importance through quantity
Moon levels contain unique animated enemies visually distinct from earlier stages; volcano has specialized threats
Spinner blocks reverse gravity visually; zippers appear as level-specific navigation tools; boat serves dual mechanic/transportation purpose

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Remains stationary at clubhouse; provides directional information about Hamburglar sightings; acknowledges completed objectives
Sighted multiple locations; flees before confrontation; reveals bag has escaped during final encounter
Launches projectiles (flag, wand, rabbit); requires defeat to complete quest; represents non-humanoid antagonist type
Stationary at specific locations; hand over puzzle cards upon collection; provide narrative direction for next stage
Mobile within levels; throwable targets; present danger through proximity; defeated via block-throwing mechanic
Collect puzzle cards; navigate hazards; throw blocks at enemies; operate unique mechanics (spinner, zipper, boat)

notes: M.C. Kids uses McDonald's brand IP as primary world-state signaling system. Character placement and location progression create linear narrative path despite non-linear level structure. Danger is primarily mechanical (enemies, hazards) rather than thematic. The game lacks explicit faction conflict—Hamburglar is sole antagonist rather than representative of opposing faction. Visual communication relies heavily on character recognition (McDonald's IP familiarity) rather than abstract danger indicators. Puzzle card collection serves dual purpose: mechanically required for progression and visually indicates quest completion. Regional differences (title change to McDonaldland in EU, publisher splash screen change) did not affect core manifestation layer. Unused beehive and bird enemy sprites indicate design iteration but do not impact released version's world-state communication.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - M.C. Kids article)
